message:
fix(sync/base-snapshot): detect merge-conflict markers in beads.base.jsonl before serde parse

Companion to the earlier "refuse to auto-flush / overwrite a JSONL that
still holds merge-conflict markers" change. `load_base_snapshot` parsed
`beads.base.jsonl` with serde as soon as the file existed, so a user
who had committed the base snapshot (against the default .gitignore)
and then hit a botched `git merge` would get a cryptic "Invalid JSON
in base snapshot at line N" buried several frames deep in the merge
stack — masking the underlying "merge conflict markers left in the
file" problem.

src/sync/mod.rs
- In `load_base_snapshot`, immediately after `require_valid_sync_path`
  validates the resolved path, call `ensure_no_conflict_markers(path)`
  so `<<<<<<<` / `=======` / `>>>>>>>` regions in the base snapshot
  surface the same actionable diagnostic the main JSONL path already
  emits.

tests/e2e_errors.rs
- New `e2e_sync_merge_detects_conflict_markers_in_base_snapshot`:
  seeds a valid JSONL, flushes to produce a clean base snapshot,
  rewrites the base snapshot with `<<<<<<< HEAD ... ======= ...
  >>>>>>> branch` regions, runs `br sync --merge`, and asserts the
  command exits non-zero with the conflict-markers diagnostic
  (not the "Invalid JSON in base snapshot" parse error).

Cargo.lock
- Sync-through of the `bumpalo` transitive dependency introduced by
  `fsqlite-pager` in the sibling frankensqlite repo's latest commit
  (shows up here because the workspace resolves through a path
  dependency and was re-locked).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
